figure.title: End-to-End Data Protection Mechanisms
figure.purpose: Illustrates the different error detection and correction mechanisms used at various stages of the UALink data path.

technical_summary: Internal to the Accelerator (UPLI to TL): Uses Parity.
Link between Accelerator and Switch (TL to TL): Uses Flit level FEC (Forward Error Correction) and CRC.
Internal to the Switch (TL to Switch Core): Uses Parity.
Inside the Switch Core: Uses Switch RAS Policy (ECC or Parity).

components.name: Parity
components.description: Used for internal data paths (e.g., between UPLI and TL).

components.name: FEC and CRC
components.description: Used for the external physical links between nodes to provide robust error detection and correction.

components.name: ECC or Parity
components.description: Used within the Switch Core based on its specific RAS policy.

critical_facts: Data protection varies by domain: internal paths use lightweight parity, external links use robust FEC/CRC, and switch cores may use ECC or parity.
